text: The surgeon mobilizes the omentum from the transverse colon, dividing the loose attachments between the posterior gastric wall and the anterior surface of the pancreas. The hepatic flexure of the colon is freed from its peritoneal attachments, exposing the pancreatic head and duodenum.

The surgeon incises the visceral peritoneum along the inferior border of the pancreas, starting from the left of the middle colic vessel and moving toward the patient's right and inferiorly. This exposes the trunk of the middle colic vein and the superior mesenteric vein (SMV), where several tributaries are visible draining directly into the SMV.
